leaderboard.note: No Holo number is published for Mind2Web. Reference Ele.Acc figures come from HTML-candidate / viewport-screenshot protocols; this smoke test uses the harsher FULL-PAGE screenshot + strict point-in-box, so Ele.Acc is a lower bound. median_dist_to_box and near_acc show how close near-misses land.

MiniWoB++ = 125 tiny synthetic GUI tasks (classic web-agent grounding benchmark). Strong supervised/specialist agents (CC-Net, WebGUM, Pix2Act, Synapse) reach ~90-96% with task-specific training; zero/few-shot LLM-vision agents (no MiniWoB training) are far lower. This is a single-model, screenshot+coordinate, zero-shot Holo3 run on a CPU NUC — a generalist probe.

Single-model, screenshot+coordinate agent (BrowserGym coord) on a 3-task shopping slice, strict must_include grading, 15-step budget, CPU-only. Result: 0/3 — the agent navigates competently (search, scroll, open product/review/order pages) and returns coherent answers, but misses on completeness/precision: it found 1 of 5 required reviewer names, gave a plausible-but-wrong price range, and ran out of steps on the spend-aggregation task. WebArena SOTA = Surfer 2 (H Company) 69.6% pass@1 — a MULTI-model stack (Claude Sonnet 4.5 navigator + o3 orchestrator + Holo1.5-72B localizer). A solo 35B-A3B at Q4 on CPU is a much harder setting; Holo3's edge shows in grounding (ScreenSpot above), not solo long-horizon web reasoning.

deferred_live_env: TheAgentCompany (OpenHands code-agent — mismatched for a coordinate-only vision model)
deferred_note: WebArena (shopping) IS run here (its own panel). Remaining live boards stay infra-gated: WebVoyager / AndroidWorld need a live browser / Android emulator; TheAgentCompany runs on OpenHands (bash + element-id actions, ~47% code-centric tasks) which a coordinate-only vision model cannot drive. OSWorld, the live computer-use board, was run separately (Holo3 = #1 open-weight).
